title: Assembly Basement
shortDescription: Bits, bytes, and getting the machine to listen to you for the first time.
longDescription: The lowest room in Meridian Computing Laboratory, where the old teletype terminals and the first compiler tapes are stored. Nothing here has run in years. Restoring it means learning what a computer actually is underneath (bits, bytes, and the toolchain that turns text you write into something the machine can execute) and getting your very first program to run.
